data.settleTest: The venture's actual sales cycle documentation for large-operator qualification (AVL/RVL gating timelines) plus a benchmark dataset of first-year market capture for comparable DC-hardware entrants selling direct to large operators — all knowable today, not dependent on future market unfolding.

proposedCorrection.rationale: Generic SOM benchmarks (1–5%) ignore that large-operator qualification cycles (NVIDIA AVL/RVL) exceed the 12-month horizon; a hardware-specific Year-1 capture for the slowest buyers is closer to 0.1–1%, so the 3% mid-band should be revised down ~5–10x.

researchQueries.query: Documented first-year market capture rates for rack-PDU or CDU hardware entrants selling direct to hyperscale/large data-center operators through NVIDIA AVL/RVL qualification
Cases of new data-center hardware vendors closing 3% of a regional serviceable market within 12 months via direct sales to large operators despite qualification gates
